page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-4GB is a virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. The system is powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This CPU configuration operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation model, delivering 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core and a 16 MB L3 cache. The server is bundled with 50 GB of local SSD storage and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and high single-threaded throughput in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression fall into average and weak categories respectively, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is mixed, with large-block read and write bandwidth performing at average levels, while small-block cached read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores register as weak to poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this server as a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU resources. It is well-suited for lightweight applications, static web serving, basic single-threaded processing tasks, and development or testing environments where high peak single-core performance is occasionally needed but sustained multi-core execution is not critical.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-4GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server features a 2.0 GHz clock speed and 16 MB of L3 cache. Benchmark data highlights exceptional single-core CPU performance, placing it in the top tier for single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, its shared CPU allocation results in average multi-core performance and weaker memory bandwidth under specific workloads. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance offers a cost-effective resource profile for workloads that benefit from strong single-thread bursts without requiring dedicated multi-core execution. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight single-threaded applications, and development environments.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and local SSD storage for lightweight workloads.

Includes 50 GB of local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
